context_for_errors: animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Denmark. Additional description: Conventional dairy. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary of the conventional dairy production system in Denmark includes:1. Farm activities related to milk production2. On-farm and off-farm feeds production3. Purchase of feeds (concentrates, fodders, silages, cereals, proteic feeds)4. Purchase of bedding materials (straw, corn stalks, sawdust)5. Production of manure from animal breeding6. Purchase of productive agricultural factors required for on-farm feeds production (seeds, fertilizers, pesticides)7. Transport of animal feed and milk products8. Emissions from manure (raw or composted)9. Major building materials for farm and processing plant buildings (amortized over 50 years for farm buildings and 30 years for milk plant buildings)10. Employee transport and corporate office operation.

animal housing producing Eggs, hen, in shell (number) in Thailand. Additional description: CB8-Parent farm 5. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System Boundaries:*** The system boundaries include all relevant material and energy inputs and emissions from the cradle-to-customer supply chains of Canadian egg and egg products in 2012.* The system includes:+ Breeder Flock Facilities (chicks, feed, water, energy, manure management, spent hen disposal)+ Layer Facilities (housing system type and characteristics, management practices, on-farm resource use, hen performance)+ Transport (inputs and emissions from transport of inputs and outputs)+ Grading Facilities (grading, washing, packaging)+ Retail (distribution to retailers)+ Egg Breaking and Further Processing Facilities (production of liquid whole egg, egg white, or egg yolk products)**Functional Unit (FU):*** The FU is 1 kg of eggs leaving the farm gate.**Subsystems:*** Concentrate and Litter Production* Rearing Hen Husbandry (including hatching)* Laying Hen Husbandry* Transport**Ecological Indicators:*** Global Warming Potential (GWP)* Energy Use (EU)* Land Occupation (LO)* Fossil Phosphorus Use

permanent pasture producing Beef cattle in Brazil. Additional description: Rerfence system where feeding is performed only by grazing anfd the supply of mineral supplementation.. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System boundary:** The system boundary includes the entire production process from birth to slaughter, including:* Breeding and reproduction* Grazing and feeding* Manure management* Land use and soil management* Feed production (grains, silage, and mineral supplements)* Energy production (e.g., electricity, fuel)* Water use and management* Waste management* Slaughter and processing**Subsystems:*** Extensive (EXT) system: no fencing, cattle grazed in the same manner, no supplementation* Semi-intensive (Semi-INT) system: cultivated pastures with Brachiaria and supplemented with mineral salts* Fattening system: cattle fattened on a mix of outdoor grazing and indoor feeding of grass silage and concentrates**Inputs:*** Feed (grains, silage, mineral supplements)* Water* Energy (electricity, fuel)* Manure* Land use and soil management* Mineral supplements**Outputs:*** Beef meat* Manure* Waste (e.g., silage, feed waste)* Greenhouse gas emissions (GHGs)* Water pollution (e

agri-food processor producing Ripened cheese in Spain. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary of this production system includes:1. Dairy farm:   - Agricultural activities related to animal feed production (maize silage, ryegrass silage, straw, and concentrates)  - Raw milk production  - Transportation of raw milk to the dairy factory2. Dairy factory:   - Milk treatment  - Coagulation and moulding  - Salting and smoking  - Packaging3. Waste treatment: - Treatment processes related to waste management of packaging materials and sludge from the WWTP (Wastewater Treatment Plant)

producing Broiler chicken in Russia. Additional description: Poultry production under the conditions of the central region in european Russia. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the life cycle assessment (LCA) relevant information for the broiler chicken production system:**System Boundaries:*** Chicken farm* Slaughterhouse* Feed production line (including water treatment and steam production)* Packaging process (including packaging materials production and distribution)* Transportation of chickens from farm to slaughterhouse**Excluded Processes:*** Production of cleaning agents (detergents and disinfectants)* Production of vaccines and antibiotics* Solid-stream processing (activities related to processing of organic residues)* Production of capital goods and infrastructures**Included Processes:*** Reception of chicks at the farm gate* Fattening period of 34 days* Production of chicken feed (maize, wheat, soybean cake, soybean oil, monocalcium phosphate, protein concentrate, and fats)* Vaccination and antibiotic administration* Cleaning activities at the chicken farm* Wastewater treatment in a small WWTP on the chicken farm* Slaughterhouse activities (killing, scalding, evisceration, and packaging)* Transportation of chickens from farm to slaughterhouse

permanent pasture producing Meat, dairy cattle (liveweight) in Brazil. Additional description: Aggregate cycle representing data from 4 farms in the Central-East mesoregion on Parana in 2019, all producing milk under a semi-confinement system, where animals spend part of each day on pasture, and the remainder confined. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary of this production system includes:* All processes from cradle to farm gate* All main production inputs:+ Dairy herd (lactating and dry cows, heifers, calves, and bulls)+ Local feeds (pasture, mineral salts, and non-local feeds)+ Fertilizers, pesticides, and machinery+ Drinking water for cattle+ Ancillary inputs (fuels, electricity, and seeds)* Excluded:+ Equipment (roads, irrigation infrastructure, buildings, and tools)+ Medicines and seeds (due to lack of unit processes in LCA databases)+ Carbon sequestration and biogenic carbon emissions of locally produced feeds (vegetation cycles shorter than one year)+ Land use change for on-farm feed production (no changes in land management practices)

agri-food processor producing Cheese, cow in Italy. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary includes:1. Feed production on supplier farms2. Transport of animal feed and milk products3. Milk processing at the dairy factory4. Emissions from manure (raw or composted) until export5. Major building materials for farm and processing plant buildings6. Employee transport and corporate office operation7. Local milk production system (from cradle to farm gate)8. Dairy farm activities (animal feed production, milk production, and other inputs)9. Dairy factory activities (milk treatment, cheese production, and packaging)10. Waste treatment and disposal of packaging materials and sludge from WWTP11. Raw milk transport between dairy farm and dairy factory12. Emissions from livestock (CH4 and N20) and manure storage (NH3)13. Carbon sequestration and biogenic carbon emissions of locally produced feeds (not considered in this study)

animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Spain. Additional description: Pasture-based milk cycle. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary of the animal housing producing milk production system in Spain includes:* Farm activities related to milk production* On-farm and off-farm feeds production* Water and energy resources* Purchase of feeds (concentrates, fodders, silages, cereals, proteic feeds)* Purchase of bedding materials (straw, corn stalks, sawdust)* Production of manure from animal breeding* Production of ancillary inputs such as fuels and electricity* Drinking water for the cattle* Farm infrastructure (roads, irrigation infrastructure, buildings, tools)* Non-local feeds with related production and transport processes* Carbon sequestration and biogenic carbon emissions of locally produced feeds (alfalfa and maize) are not considered due to vegetation cycles shorter than one year.The system does not include:* Equipment of the farms with specialized tools* Medicines and seeds* Emissions from land use change for the production of on-farm feeds* Emissions from land use change for purchased feed production* Emissions from land use change for soybeans in Brazil* Emissions from animal respiration and CO2 fixed by plants through photosynthesis* Employee transport and corporate office operation* Manure leaving the farm without

animal housing producing Broiler chicken, hen in Thailand. Additional description: CB2-Parent farm 1. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System Boundaries:*** The system includes:+ Animal housing (broiler chicken production)+ Feed production+ Vaccines and cleaning production+ Slaughterhouse+ Packaging process* Excluded from the system boundaries:+ Solid-stream processing (outside the slaughterhouse)+ Production of cleaning agents (outside the slaughterhouse)+ Composting of manure (outside the system boundaries)+ Production of animal drugs and chicks (outside the system boundaries)**Functional Unit:*** 1 kg Carcass Weight (CW) is used as the functional unit for the LCA assessment.**Life Cycle Stages:*** Upstream: Chick production and feed production* Downstream: Slaughterhouse, packaging, and distribution**Control Volumes:*** Distinct control volumes for the Grower (who owns and operates the houses) and the Integrator (who owns the birds and feed)* Combined control volume that encompasses the overall costs and life cycle impacts for the entire broiler production system.
